name: FormControl
summary: Wraps a form to provide a typed store context that field components bind to by `name`; use it to collect and submit grouped field values without lifting every field into local state. Also carries form-wide config — `disabled`, name-keyed `errors`, and an async-submit busy state — shared with bound fields via `useFormConfig()`.

props.name: useStore
props.description:
The **Zustand hook** produced by `createFormStore(initialValues)`.
It’s the usual `const useForm = createFormStore(...)` function.

props.name: onSubmitValues
props.description:
Fires after native submit is intercepted – gives you typed values. May
return a Promise; while it is pending the form is `aria-busy` and every
bound field sees `isSubmitting` via `useFormConfig()`.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disable every bound field in this form.

props.name: errors
props.description: Name-keyed error messages; a bound field whose `name` matches shows the error.

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

bestPractices: FormControl prevents native submit by default. Handle submission in `onSubmitValues(values, event)` and keep the flow inside your SPA.
Always provide `name` on fields within a FormControl so values bind to the store and appear in the typed payload.

Disable the whole form at once with `disabled` (e.g. while saving) — every bound field reads it and a field's own `disabled` still wins. Each field merges form config with its own props (own value wins, form config is the fallback).

Surface validation by passing a name-keyed `errors` map (`{ email: 'Invalid' }`); the matching bound field shows the error and is marked `aria-invalid`. After a submit, focus moves to the first invalid field.
Return a Promise from `onSubmitValues` for async submits: the form goes `aria-busy` and bound fields see `isSubmitting` (via `useFormConfig()`) until it settles.
For files or non‑valet inputs, merge `new FormData(event.currentTarget)` with your typed `values` in `onSubmitValues`.
If you depend on browser autofill, verify final values at submit time by reading `FormData` to avoid stale store state when no change events fired.

examples.title: Typed form with onSubmitValues
Merging typed values with FormData (files, autofill)
